headline: Agency performance ratings: 92% scored 'excellent' despite AG findings
context: The Performance Management and Delivery Unit rated 92% of federal agencies as 'excellent' in 2025, contradicting the Auditor General's critical findings on the same agencies.

cards.big: 92% of agencies rated 'excellent' — while the AG flagged most of them for failures
cards.sub: Internal ratings and external audits tell completely contradictory stories

cards.big: Star rating system uses self-reported data with no independent verification
cards.sub: Agencies grade their own homework — and 92% give themselves top marks

cards.big: Agencies rated excellent received RM1.2 billion in performance incentive bonuses
cards.sub: Inflated ratings directly translate into additional public spending on bonuses

cards.big: AG flagged 68% of 'excellent-rated' agencies for financial irregularities in same period
cards.sub: Excellence ratings coexist with documented governance failures in the same agencies

cards.big: When everyone is excellent, the rating system isn't measuring performance — it's distributing bonuses
Should agency performance ratings be independently assessed using AG audit findings?
